title: Gooey Brazilian Bamboo Shoot Stir-fry
description: This low-calorie-friendly one-pot Brazilian dish features fermented bamboo shoots that become gooey and delicious when stir-fried with a blend of aromatic vegetables.
instructions: Wash bamboo shoots and soak them in water for at least 30 minutes to remove any bitterness. Cut into bite-sized pieces.
Peel onions, ginger, and garlic. Finely chop all vegetables and set aside.
Heat oil in a large skillet or wok over medium heat. Add chopped vegetables and sauté until slightly softened.
Add bamboo shoots, soy sauce, vinegar, salt, and pepper to the skillet. Stir-fry for another 5 minutes until the bamboo shoots are cooked through but still tender.
Adjust seasoning if necessary and serve immediately.
